full_text:
TMA for antibody validations

The lab has a tissue microarray (TMA) block “PANCAN_CTRL_5” containing cores from a range of human tissues, including both benign and malignant samples.

A - Samples and outline of TMA block

Core mapping:

Malignant tissue

Benign tissue

Tissues:

A1 Lung adenocarcinoma
A2 Lung squamous cell carcinoma

A3 Colon carcinoma
A4 Ductal breast carcinoma
A5 Ovarian carcinoma
A6 Squamous cell carcinoma, oral mucosa

B3 Tonsil
B4 Spleen
B5 Lymph node
B6 Kidney
B7 Appendix
B8 Lung

C3 Liver
C4 Fallopian tube; premenopausal
C5 Fallopian tube; postmenopausal
C6 Testis
C7 Benign basal regions of squamous epithelium
C8 Placenta

B - Categorization of tissues

Lymphatic tissues → tonsil, spleen, lymph node

High amount of immune cells

Positive for many immune cell markers

Epithelial tissues → lung, liver, kidney, appendix

Diverse epithelium

For validating epithelial markers

Adenocarcinomas → lung adenocarcinoma, colon carcinoma, ductal breast carcinoma, ovarian carcinoma

Benign squamous epithelium → benign basal regions of squamous epithelium

Squamous cell carcinomas → lung squamous cell carcinoma, oral mucosa squamous cell carcinoma

Reproductive tissues → testis, fallopian tube (pre+postmenopausal)

Hormone-related markers

Specialized tissues → placenta

C - Guidelines for using validation TMA block

A1 | A2 | A3 | A4 | A5 | A6 | - | -
- | - | B3 | B4 | B5 | B6 | B7 | B8
- | - | C3 | C4 | C5 | C6 | C7 | C8
